old_claim.note: Tribalism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The source explains early possession through concentrated resources and sedentism rather than a required tribal political form.

source_shape.source_locator: Article summary: family-based private-property institutions occurred at some rich resource sites before the first farmers; sedentism and farming later broadened those norms.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Family-based possession norms could precede farming and later spread with sedentism and cultivation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. The source explains early possession through concentrated resources and sedentism rather than a required tribal political form.

ontology_before: property_concepts treated tribalism as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: property_concepts is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims tribalism as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: property_concepts no longer directly depends on tribalism.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current property_concepts scope technically requires tribalism.
short_reason: The source explains early possession through concentrated resources and sedentism rather than a required tribal political form.
